name: libspreadsheet-writeexcel-perl
title: libspreadsheet-writeexcel-perl
description:
module to create Excel spreadsheets
Spreadsheet::WriteExcel is a Perl module that can be used to create a cross-
platform Excel binary file. Multiple worksheets can be added to a workbook
and formatting can be applied to cells. Additionally, this module supports
writing text, numeric values, formulas and hyperlinks to cells.
.
The Excel file produced by this module is compatible with Excel 5, 95, 97
and 2000. Generated files are also compatible with the Linux/UNIX spreadsheet
applications OpenOffice.org, Gnumeric and XESS. The generated files are not
compatible with MS Access.
.
This module cannot be used to read an Excel file. See Spreadsheet::ParseExcel
(libspreadsheet-parseexcel-perl) or look at the main documentation for some
suggestions. This module cannot be used to write to an existing Excel file.

commands.name: chartex
commands.description: A utility to extract charts from an Excel file for insertion into a Spreadsheet::WriteExcel file.

arguments.description: The root name for the extracted charts,
arguments.raw: --chartname  The root name for the extracted charts,

arguments.description: Print a brief help message and exits.
arguments.raw: --help  Print a brief help message and exits.

arguments.description: Prints the manual page and exits.
arguments.raw: --man  Prints the manual page and exits.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use chartex because module to create Excel spreadsheets.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use chartex as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `chartex [--chartname --help --man] file.xls` as documented upstream for chartex.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair chartex with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `chartex --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
